回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	113學年度第1學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 英語語音訓練 (English Pronunciation Drill)	開課班級 : 應英一年級	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 陳宛利	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
1. To enhance students' ability to convey ideas clearly and effectively in English. 2. To build self-confidence and reduce anxiety when speaking English. 3. To improve pronunciation and fluency through immersive practice. 4. To develop quick-thinking and adaptability in real-life conversation scenarios. 5. To foster engaging and dynamic communication skills using drama and dialogue techniques. 


2.教學綱要
The goal of this course is to provide students with the tools they need to communicate clearly in English. It is aimed to promote clear, effective communication and to build the students’ self-confidence and comfort when speaking English. To achieve this, the course incorporates elements of drama and dialogue, immersing students in role-playing activities, scripted conversations, and improvisational scenarios. These techniques not only make learning engaging and fun but also simulate real-life situations, enabling students to practice and perfect their speaking skills in a dynamic and supportive environment.

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Assessment: attendance, class participation & presentation, quiz (40%); Midterm Exam (30%); Final Exam (30%) 
Course Requirements: Students are expected to attend all the class sessions and attend each class with the textbook. Late arrival, coming to class empty-handed, or being a phubber in class will lead to a deduction of the final grade. Be on time and come prepared to learn and contribute. Students will be selected at random to answer questions and be graded based on how well they demonstrate understandings of the reading. No make-up quizzes for your absences. Understand and be responsible for your grade. 
To facilitate learning outside the classroom, course materials and assignments will be uploaded onto E-campus, an E-learning environment for all NQU courses. All students must resister for E-campus and also enter a frequently used email account in order to gain full access to course materials, to receive course updates, to form small groups, and to submit homework online. In an early prevention of an unexpected internet breakdown, please be advised to download weekly reading materials in advance. For course-related questions, contact me at tmpmlily@gmail.com * 詳盡之授課規劃將於第一週上課公告及說明，並依照實際上課狀況與(遠距)教學形式作適度調整，請同學至Ecampus平台，課程內輸入Email信箱，以接收即時課程資訊。
